capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize a scoped status comparison from full records.
prompt:
The site coordinator is checking two facilities requests for the maintenance handoff. Create `maintenance_handoff.md` using evidence from full records rather than search summaries. Find the request named “Atrium Lighting Repair” at “Building A” and the request named “Training Room Setup” at “Building B”.

Use the genuine local `./facilitydesk` executable and inspect its built-in help before acting. Do not read the backing database, SQL seed, protected verifier, or generated action receipt directly. Your first facilities operation must issue both exact name-and-location searches together in one Pi shell-tool action. Do not perform either search earlier or include any other facilities operation in that action. Wait for both results. Then treat each branch independently: retrieve it only when its search produced exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, issue both full-record gets together in the next Pi shell-tool action. Never infer or guess an ID, date, or status. Search results are abbreviated, so take dates and statuses only from successful full-record gets. The executable performs paired work concurrently and records the staged operations for protected verification.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while still completing any independently resolved branch. Do not expose unrelated records, extra full-record fields, or proposed changes.

The handoff must contain exactly three nonblank lines and no heading or other prose. In the requested request order, write two Markdown bullets shaped `- <name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status>`, followed by exactly `Status comparison: <same|different>.` Use `same` or `different` only when both statuses came from successful full-record gets. For an unresolved branch, keep its requested name and location, write `UNRESOLVED` for its stable ID, date, and status, and use `Status comparison: unavailable.` Run `python3 protected_tests/verify.py` when finished. The acceptance files are protected.
